fuel_legend.label: NFFL 9: Hardwood litter, loose and fluffy (broadleaf-open)
NFFL 10: Timber with understory, large downed logs (conifer-closed)

exposure_assets.description: Scored assets (subset of the ScoredAsset columns; authoritative GeoParquet with full per-row provenance is the STAC asset)

fuel_class.description: Fuel-class COG display copy (warped from the authoritative EPSG:32629 STAC asset, NEAREST, no resolution loss)

burn_scar.description: Burn-scar inference-probability COG display copy (warped from the authoritative EPSG:4326 STAC/R2 asset, NEAREST)

icnf_burns.description: ICNF Áreas Ardidas perimeters (1990–2025 vintages inside the AOI)

exposure_assets_iberia.description: Merged full-extent exposure assets across 16646 rows / all study areas; coloured by impact_severity = within-AOI exposure x asset criticality, normalised across study areas (NOT an absolute cross-region risk or probability). Per-AOI exposure_rank stays AOI-relative.

input_ramps.label: Canopy height
input_ramps.caption: Sentinel-2 / GEDI canopy height (metres). Taller, denser canopy is more fuel-loaded — a relative model input, not a probability.

input_ramps.label: Slope
input_ramps.caption: Terrain slope (degrees, from the Copernicus DEM). Steeper slopes carry fire faster uphill — a relative model input, not a probability.

input_ramps.label: NBR-delta (ΔNBR)
input_ramps.caption: Change in Normalized Burn Ratio over the window: positive (red) = vegetation loss / burn-severity signal, negative (green) = regrowth. A relative spectral input, not a probability and not a fire forecast.

fwi_overlay.attribution: Source: CEMS Early Warning Data Store — Copernicus / ECMWF (CC-BY-4.0)

firescope.attribution: FireScope relative wildfire-risk rank (CC-BY-4.0) — INSAIT-Institute + ETH, arXiv:2511.17171
firescope.caption: Relative wildfire-risk RANK (SOTA reference): a 0–254 relative-risk rank over Iberia. A reference layer to read the exposure rank against — NOT a probability and NOT a forecast. FireScope relative wildfire-risk rank (CC-BY-4.0) — INSAIT-Institute + ETH, arXiv:2511.17171

burn_history.caption: Observed historical burned-area perimeters across Iberia, styled by source. Portugal (ICNF) is fine-resolution and goes back to 1990; Spain (EFFIS) is coarser and only from 2016 — a real PT/ES temporal + resolution asymmetry, not a modelling artefact. Observed history, not a probability or forecast.

mosaics.caption: Burn-scar mosaic temporarily withdrawn — de-grid fix in progress. The Prithvi-EO-2.0 burn-scar inference is being re-run to remove a ViT-tiling grid artefact; it will return once the de-gridded composite is published. Recent-scar DETECTION — spectral signatures of fires that already happened — NOT a forecast or ignition prediction (non-negotiable #6). A relative model score, not a calibrated probability.

Change in Normalized Burn Ratio (ΔNBR) over the scoring window across the pilot + 4 study areas, all tiles shown at once. Positive (red) = vegetation loss / burn-severity signal; negative (green) = regrowth. A relative spectral input, not a probability and not a fire forecast. Loads on toggle from R2.
